**Mint** is a large group of aromatic herbs used as flavoring.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)

## Characteristics

[edit | edit source]

Generally, mints are green with strong and cooling flavor profiles,[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) though secondary aromas (e.g. fruity) are found among the varieties.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)

### Varieties

[edit | edit source]

There are many varieties of mint for culinary use,[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4)[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) though two very prevalent ones are **spearmint** and **peppermint**.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6)[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7) The former is *Mentha spicata*,[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6) which features broad, toothed, bright green leaves and a wrinkled texture.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[8]](#cite_note-:7-8)**[[9]](#cite_note-:8-9)** Its aroma is characterized by the compound R-carvone, along with other less-abundant compounds.[[10]](#cite_note-:9-10) Peppermint (*Mentha x piperita*), on the other hand, has thin, stiff leaves and red/purple-tinged stems.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)**[[9]](#cite_note-:8-9)** Its flavor is strongly pungent and dominated by menthol,[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6)[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4) which lends a cooling sensation.[[10]](#cite_note-:9-10)[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)[[11]](#cite_note-:73-11)

A number of other mint varieties exist, and they are summarized in the below table:

| Species | Common name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| *Mentha x gracilis* or *Mentha x gentilis[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6)* | Ginger mint, Scotch spearmint, red mint | Hybrid of spearmint and field mint; warm flavor.[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6)[[9]](#cite_note-:8-9) |
| *Mentha aquatica* | Water mint[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) | Strong aroma[[10]](#cite_note-:9-10) |
| *Mentha aquatica var. citrata* | Bergamot mint | Aroma reminiscent of citrus.[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) |
| *Mentha arvensis* | Field mint, corn mint, Japanese mint, wild mint[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)**[[9]](#cite_note-:8-9)** | Significant source of menthol.[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) |
| *Mentha longifolia* | Horsemint[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) |  |
| *Mentha x piperita var. piperita* | Black peppermint | Variety of peppermint with dark stems and leaves.[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6) |
| *Mentha requienii* | Spanish mint, Corsican mint, crème de menthe plant[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) | Small leaves.[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) |
| *Mentha spicata var. crispa* | Moroccan mint | Variety of spearmint; flavor is slightly spicy.[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6) |
| *Mentha suaveolens* (*Mentha rotundifolia*) | Apple mint, round-leaf mint, woolly mint[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[9]](#cite_note-:8-9) | Hairy leaves, slight apple aroma.[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6) |

## Selection and storage

[edit | edit source]

Mint can be purchased fresh or dried.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) Fresh mint should be stored in the fridge. Dried mint should be stored in an airtight container away from light, heat, and moisture.

## Use

[edit | edit source]

Generally, mints are used in a variety of cuisines and for various purposes.[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6) Spearmint, for example, is used in preserves, sauces, beverages, and more in Europe, North Africa, South Asian, and Middle Eastern cuisines.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)[[10]](#cite_note-:9-10) It is also used as a garnish.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[12]](#cite_note-:6-12) Field mint is used in various Asian cuisines.[[9]](#cite_note-:8-9) Peppermint is often used in sweet preparations, such as confections, chocolates, and ice creams.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)[[8]](#cite_note-:7-8)[[9]](#cite_note-:8-9) Generally, peppermint and more subtly aromatic varieties are not added early in cooking, since they can evaporate or degrade with long and strong heat.[[6]](#cite_note-:3-6)[[10]](#cite_note-:9-10) Mints may be processed into extracts and essential oils for use in cooking.[[9]](#cite_note-:8-9)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Substitution

[edit | edit source]

The different varieties of mint may have similar properties,[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) but they are not completely interchangeable. The difference in flavor will be notable when swapping.
